The '''Sabotage at Rhen Var''' occurred on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The battle followed a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Attack on Rhen Var|attack]] that [[Ground warfare/Legends|captured]] an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Rhen Var Imperial outpost/Legends|listening outpost]] on Rhen Var. The Galactic Empire sent a force to recapture it, which succeeded, although the resulting clash led to the destruction of the [[Listening post/Legends|outpost]] and the defeat of all the Rebel forces.

==Prelude==
The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had long operated a [[Listening post/Legends|listening outpost]] out of a derelict [[Rhen Var Citadel|citadel]] on [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]],<ref name="bf" /> an [[Water/Legends|ice]]-covered [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Atlas" /> deeply entrenched in history surrounding the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="CW">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]]</ref> the precursor government to the Empire.<ref name="Atlas" /> In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]<ref name="Date" />—a conflict between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] freedom fighters—the outpost was able to monitor [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel fleet]] activity. The Rebel Alliance subsequently launched an [[Attack on Rhen Var|assault]] to prevent the outpost from tracking them.<ref name="bf" /> The Rebels [[Ground warfare/Legends|captured]] the facility from the Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] stationed there and maintained control over it for a few [[Standard day/Legends|days]] before the Empire launched a counterattack.<ref name="Atlas" />

==The sabotage==
The Imperial attack force—consisting of [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtroopers]], [[Vehicle Command Crewer|Imperial Army pilots]], [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout troopers]], [[Imperial shock trooper/Legends|shock troopers]], and [[Phase Zero dark trooper]]s—arrived and was ordered to seize the section of the outpost called the Keep. The Imperials advanced against the Rebels, who had deployed at a tower below the station. The Rebel force was comprised of [[Rebel trooper/Legends|troopers]], [[Rebel Vanguard/Legends|vanguard division soldiers]], [[Rebel Marksman/Legends|snipers]], [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]].<ref name="bf" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Battlefront}}
Though both sides defended their positions with [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser turrets]], the Imperials retook the Keep.<ref name="bf" />
{{Endgame}}

The Imperial counterattack overran the Alliance forces,<ref name="bf" /> with the strike ultimately ending in the destruction of the outpost and the loss of all Rebel forces.<ref name="Atlas" />

==Aftermath==
By the end of the battle, all civilized presence on the planet had been wiped out.<ref name="Atlas" /> Shortly thereafter, the Rebel Alliance faced the Empire in an [[Raid on Yavin 4|assault]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]],<ref name="bf" /> leading up to the pivotal [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="Date" /> in which the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] was destroyed.<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Our long-standing listening outpost on the high peaks of Rhen Var is under Rebel attack. This outpost is key to monitoring the local systems for Rebel activity. Defend the monitoring station from the Rebel attack.|[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] briefs the player before the mission|<ref name="bf" />}}
The Sabotage at Rhen Var appears in a mission of the same name in the Galactic Civil War campaign of the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''. The game delegates no specific battle plan for the player, playing from the Imperial perspective, to follow. The player is instructed to retake the Keep, which is one of the command posts, but the player can still win the level without doing so. The Imperials have 250 reinforcements available, while the Rebels have 200. When either side reaches zero, the game ends, but the Imperials must win for the campaign to continue.<ref name="bf" />

The battle features a discrepancy between the various sources. The ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' game states that the Imperial forces were defending the outpost,<ref name="bf" /> but this was overwritten by [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', which says that the Rebels captured the outpost before the Imperials retook it.<ref name="Atlas" />
